name: Tivoli Gardens
description: Copenhagen's celebrated theme park and gardens.

name: Superkilen
description: An ultra-modern diversity park in Denmark has turned an entire city block electric pink.

name: Grundtvig’s Church
description: This sublime architectural marvel is a rare example of an expressionist church.

name: Elephant Gate
description: Pre-Nazi swastikas adorn the sculpted pachyderms flanking this brewhouse portal.

name: The Little Mermaid
description: The de facto symbol of Copenhagen is this bronze fairytale which cannot seem to keep its head for very long.

name: Cisternerne
description: A 19th-century subterranean reservoir repurposed as a contemporary art cave.

name: The Genetically Modified Little Mermaid
description: Hans Christian Andersen for the postmodern world.

name: Freetown Christiania
description: Danish tourist destination well known for open stalls where vendors sell marijuana on the street.

name: Church of Our Saviour
description: Climb to the top of its spiral spire for spectacular views of Copenhagen.

name: Forgotten Giants
description: Six beautiful wooden giants are hidden around Copenhagen.

name: The Blue Planet
description: Vortex-shaped aquarium hosts Europe's largest school of piranhas and other ocean creatures.

name: Museum Obscurum
description: This secret collection of the strange and occult was rediscovered in a forgotten room of an old house.

name: Throne of Denmark
description: Opulence and fantasy collide in this royal chair which is supposedly made with unicorn horns.

name: Agnete and the Merman
description: Beneath Denmark's Højbro bridge, a bronze merman and his seven sons forever mourn for the mother and wife who left them for land.

name: Verdenskortet
description: A walkable map of the world, made from soil and stone by one man.

name: Medical Museion
description: A novel collection of medical curiosities housed in an 18th-century surgeon training academy.

name: Rundetårn
description: The old observatory in Copenhagen has a spiral corridor used as a racetrack.

name: Your Rainbow Panorama
description: This colorful artistic feature atop a Danish art gallery allows visitors to stroll through a rainbow.

name: Jens Olsen's World Clock
description: The gilded, masterful astronomical clock will calculate time, dates, and planetary positions for thousands of years to come.

name: Møns Klint
description: A natural wonder in south Denmark.

name: Kosmik Rum
description: Visitors are treated to a stained glass kaleidoscope interior inside this work of art.

name: Geological Museum
description: Museum on earth sciences featuring a recreation of a famous curiosity cabinet.

name: Nasothek Nose Collection
description: A collection of noses tucked inside an art museum reveals how times have changed.

name: Leif Sonne's Bottled Beer Collection
description: The largest bottled beer collection in the world holds over 22,000 unique beer bottles.

name: Wehrmacht Graffiti
description: Bored potato-guarding Nazis etched their thoughts onto a brick storage building in Copenhagen's Meatpacking District.

name: Kronborg Castle (Elsinore)
description: Old Danish castle immortalized in Shakespeare's Hamlet.

name: Lego House
description: An enormous playhouse designed to look like 21 stacked Lego bricks, with another 25 million colorful Legos inside.

name: Cirkelbroen (The Circle Bridge)
description: This bridge's unique architecture honors nautical heritage and encourages self-awareness.

name: Lindholm Høje
description: This Danish rock field is actually a sprawling ancient cemetery holding the bodies of 700 Vikings.

name: Tollund Man
description: Iron Age "bog person" preserved for over 2,300 years.

name: Dyrehavsbakken
description: The oldest amusement park in the world has been providing a nice spot to have some fun since the 1500s.

name: Sankt Peders Bageri
description: Denmark’s oldest bakery is home to the 'Wednesday Snail,' one of the best cinnamon rolls on the planet.

name: Trelleborg Viking Ring Fortress
description: A perfectly circular stronghold constructed in the 10th century is now a nucleus of Viking culture housing a museum, village, and reenactment of an epic Viking battle.

name: Mønsted Kalkgruber
description: Nearly 40 miles and 1,000 years of limestone mines under Denmark.

name: Samsø Labyrinten
description: The world's largest maze is a nature park with nearly 200 decision points.

name: CopenHill
description: The world's first combination ski slope and power plant.

name: Den Uendelige Bro (The Infinite Bridge)
description: Denmark’s never-ending bridge.

name: Laksegade
description: This ordinary street was made infamous by a legendary visit from the devil.

name: Legoland in Billund
description: The original home of the Lego has a huge amusement park dedicated to the iconic plastic toy.

name: Rubjerg Knude Lighthouse
description: An abandoned lighthouse awaits its destiny: being buried under shifting sand.

name: Grenen
description: The northernmost point in Denmark is a turbulent sandbar created by two clashing seas.

name: Man Meets The Sea
description: Four alabaster giants contemplate the tides on the shore of Denmark.

name: Kastrup Sea Bath
description: This ultra-modern wooden boardwalk turns the ocean into a swimming pool.

name: Land of Legends
description: This Denmark theme park is part family attraction, part experimental archaeology project.

name: Roskilde Viking Ship Museum
description: Nordic giants resurrected from the sea and restored to their former greatness.

name: Frederiksborg Castle
description: This striking royal residence is the largest Renaissance-style castle in Scandinavia.

name: Grauballe Man
description: Preserved "bog person" that is over 2,000 years old.

name: Vejrpigerne ('The Weather Girls')
description: This pair of gilded girls atop an Art Deco building in Copenhagen were built to function as a weather indicator.

name: Hans Christian Andersen's Grave
description: The final resting place of the author who gave us the Little Mermaid, the Ugly Duckling, and many more beloved fairy tales.

name: Velling Koller Fairy Tale Gardens
description: The decaying remains of a 1960s mechanical wonderland that never officially closed.

name: Voergaard Castle
description: This Danish castle is supposedly haunted by a woman too independent to not be a witch.

name: Ny Carlsberg Glyptotek Ghost Swastikas
description: The art museum is decorated with creatively remodeled pre-Nazi swastikas.

name: Jarmers Tower
description: The only surviving piece of Copenhagen's 16th-century defensive wall.

name: Jelling Viking Monuments
description: Mounds, rune stones, and a 1,000-year-old church, left behind by Denmark's first two kings.

name: Thingbæk Kalkminer
description: An old limestone mine transformed into a beautiful and unusual sculpture museum.

name: Toga Vin & Ølstue
description: An old Danish political institution where members of government can grab a drink off the record.

name: The Sand-Covered Church
description: Tiring of digging out the door to this church for every service, it was eventually abandoned to the sands.

name: Restaurant Schønnemann
description: Traditional Danish spot where the schnapps are flowing and the smørrebrød is loaded.

name: Thagaard's Plantation
description: A failed attempt to control drifting sands yielded a gnarled sylvan wonderland.

name: Hvide Sande Nazi Bunkers
description: Remnants of the Nazis' occupation of Denmark linger along the sand.

name: BonBon Land
description: The greatest amusement park in the world is wall-to-wall farting dogs, puking rats, and cows with exposed breasts for the whole family.

name: Martha and Marie
description: Long before ultrasounds and modern testing, medical specimens like these conjoined twins helped physicians learn about congenital anomalies.

name: Bispetorvet Dinosaur Footprint
description: A public display of random paleontology.

name: ZinkGlobal
description: This contemplative sculpture made out of old machine parts is meant to be the first in a series of sculptures around the world.

name: Stærekassen Passageway Mosaic
description: One of Scandinavia's largest mosaics pays tribute to Danish art and science luminaries.

name: Blåvand Beach Bunker Mules
description: These old World War II fortifications have been transformed into an unusual equine attraction.

name: 'Trold, der vejrer kristenblod' ('Troll, which Sniffs Out Christian Blood')
description: Positioned outside a church, this sinister folkloric figure incited protests in both 1902 and 2002.

name: Dragsholm Castle
description: Considered one of the most haunted places in Europe, this 800-year-old castle was home to noble prisoners.

name: Geodetic Midpoint of Denmark
description: Once a meeting point for great thinkers, and the midpoint of the country.

name: Svaneapotek
description: From buck blood to Theriak miracle tonic, this collection of Renaissance chemist's gear look's like a wizard's pantry.

name: The Singing Trees
description: Dozens of musicians have planted trees in this Danish park, and donated songs to go with them.

name: Haraldskær Woman
description: When this Iron Age bog body was discovered in 1835, it was thought that she had died recently.

name: Folketingssalen Frieze
description: An "evil painter" included pointed symbols in this artwork to poke fun at members of the Danish Parliament.

name: Niels Bohr's Grave
description: The final resting place of one of the brightest physicists of the 20th century.

name: 'Tisseren' ('The Pee-er')
description: An unconventional and naturalistic sculpture depicting the artist marking his territory on an exterior faculty wall.

name: The Wooden Man
description: Generations have refused to move this doll from its dusty resting place to preserve a castle from a watery fate.

name: The Troll Church
description: In legend, this ancient dolmen was said to have been created when a troll tried to destroy a church.

name: Submerged Sculpture of Hans Christian Andersen
description: After funding was cut for a memorial to the Danish author, the sculptor had his statue ceremonially lowered into the city harbor.

name: Viksø Helmets
description: Discovered in a peat bog, these horned helms come from a thousand years before the Viking Age.

name: Skovtårnet (Forest Tower)
description: A spiralling hourglass-shaped tower rises from the forests in Denmark.

name: Bolsjefabrikken (The Candy Factory)
description: A former smithy turned cultural hub with the capital's largest mural space.

name: Tuborgflasken
description: Danish observation tower designed to look like a giant beer bottle.

name: St. Canute's Cathedral
description: The last Viking king of Denmark is buried inside the crypt of this cathedral.

name: Østerlars Church
description: The largest of Denmark's seven mysterious round churches stands on an island in the Baltic Sea.

name: Tolbod Bodega
description: Opened in 1787, this old-world treasure is said to be the oldest restaurant in Copenhagen.

name: Hair Jewelry Exhibit at the Bangsbo Museum
description: The largest collection of hair jewelry in Europe reminds you how creepy 19th-century love was.

name: 'Havfrue' ('Mermaid')
description: Copenhagen's "other" little mermaid marks the historic location of past sea maid sightings.

name: 'Magic Mushrooms'
description: This inverted metropolis model is the artist's "revenge" for not being allowed to have a doll house as a kid.

name: Deep Forest Artland ​Skovsnogen
description: This Danish art forest places loud works of contemporary art among the pensive silence of nature.

name: 'Scenarie'
description: An artist has turned what most people would consider a bathroom nightmare into a work of political art.

name: Ørsted's House
description: The house where a Danish physicist discovered the connection between electricity and magnetism.

name: Sneglehuset (The Snail House)
description: A Danish fisherman spent 25 years covering nearly every inch of his home with tens of thousands of shells as a gift to his wife.

name: The Time Traveler’s Grave
description: Hidden in plain sight, a gravestone seemingly inscribed from the future stands among the cemetery’s historic tombs.

name: Jorck's Passage
description: Site of the first radio and telephone broadcasting companies in Copenhagen.

name: ‘Death and the Mother’
description: This sculptural representation of an Andersen fairy tale had to be pushed away for being too grim.

name: Himmelbjerget
description: Denmark's famous pseudo-hill.

name: Bølgen (The Wave)
description: This unique architectural wonder is home to luxury apartments.

name: Museumsgården
description: This antique farmhouse is one of Læsø's rare seaweed houses.

name: Nellemann's Have
description: This historic orchard contains Northern Europe's largest collection of paradise apple trees.

name: Royal Danish Library Treasures
description: Denmark’s ultra-modern library hides a treasury of rare, unique books.

name: Danmarks Jernbanemuseum (Danish Railway Museum)
description: An exhibition of preserved locomotives housed in a 1950s depot shows the history of Denmark's railway.

name: Hals Whale Jaws
description: A small Denmark town displays a pair of titanic jaw bones as their community symbol.

name: 'Han' ('He')
description: The shiny silver little brother to Copenhagen's Little Mermaid statue.

name: Elia
description: This huge industrial sculpture looks like a UFO and a factory had a baby.

name: Smiling Sun Mural
description: A symbol for the opposition to nuclear power around the world.

name: Slau's Stones
description: A whimsical sculpture garden right out of a children's storybook.

name: Tidens Samling
description: This museum is a portal to 20th-century Denmark.

name: The V House
description: This striking structure, with balconies jutting out like hedgehog spines, combine cutting-edge design with a playful approach to light and space.

name: Minibyen Sæby
description: Residents of this seaside village are building a miniature 1:10 scale version of their historic town.

name: Værnhytterne
description: This quiet bird sanctuary on the Danish coast is home to something not usually found in a conservation area: hunters' cabins.

name: Rebild National Park
description: This Danish park has celebrated the American independence day since 1912.

name: Happy City Birds Mural
description: Famous for his giant trolls, Thomas Dambo's lesser-known early work is a creative 3D mural with sustainable birdhouses.

name: Tusculanum
description: The remains of the observatory of the Danish astronomer who first measured the speed of light.

name: Pixlart
description: A beautiful photography exhibition space housed in a former church.

name: Christiansø
description: This remote island, home to the easternmost point in Denmark, once hosted pirates and a military fortress.

name: Bjørnegrotten
description: An abandoned bear enclosure from a 19th-century zoo located in a Danish forest.

name: Gamle Færgestræde
description: Just off the city’s central station hides a quaint lamp-lit alley into medieval Elsinore.

name: Cirkuskroen (The Circus Pub)
description: This legendary dive bar boasts a collection of over 1,300 clown figurines.

name: Brorfelde Observatory
description: Built after WWII, the observatory site for the University of Copenhagen exhibits once world-class instruments.

name: Memphis Mansion
description: An Elvis superfan built Denmark's own version of Graceland.

name: Hans Christian Andersen's Trunk
description: The traveling wares of Denmark's most famous author.

name: Odinstårnet (Odin Tower)
description: A model memorial to the original Odin Tower, the second highest tower in Europe before it was sabotaged by Danish Nazis.

name: Dodekalitten
description: An air of mystery pervades this modern-day singing Stonehenge.

name: Christian Jacobsen Drakenberg's Last Home
description: The final home of the legendary Danish sailor said to have lived to be 146 years old.

name: The Wind Turbine Beach
description: Along this stretch of beach in Denmark resides several massive bastions of energy.

name: 'Støvlen' ('The Boot')
description: This colossal hiking boot, once built for a scout jamboree, serves as an observation tower and barbecue area.

name: Elstedhaven
description: Faith and landscaping come together in a small Danish haven dubbed the "Bible Garden."

name: Stikhokken
description: It might look like just a pile of sticks—because it is. But there's a dark history to how this stack got its start.

name: Klitmøller Beach
description: On a stretch of Danish coast known as "Cold Hawaii," this fishing village has become a surfing town.

name: Flyvestation Værløse (Flight Station Værløse)
description: This World War II airfield enjoys a second life as a public park.

name: Whale Jawbone Fence
description: Danish whalers once brought back whale bones to use as building materials in places where wood was scarce.

name: Aalborg Tower
description: This modernist tower was saved by a fraternity with a 500-year-old penchant for shooting at wooden parrots.

name: 'Stoppenålen' ('The Darning Needle')
description: This risqué sculpture, based on a Hans Christian Andersen fairy tale, caused outrage 35 years after it was erected.

name: ‘Liden Gunver’
description: A somber statue commemorating a Danish merfolk-tale.

name: Græshoppebroen (Grasshopper Bridge)
description: This distinctive bug-like bridge is made of recycled horseshoes.

name: Hammershus Castle Ruins
description: Northern Europe’s largest castle ruins sit precariously on an island cliffside.

name: Nørrelandskirkens Klokketårn (Church of Nørreland Bell Tower)
description: A unique, modernist church tower that appears to float above the ground.

name: Cloos Tower
description: Plans for this local observation tower were debated for so long, the allocated funds lost value and the design had to be changed.

name: 'The Little Match Girl'
description: With a bundle of matches in one hand and a mobile phone in the other, this sculpture is a modern take on Hans Christian Andersen's poignant fairy tale.

name: 'Metaforkirkegården' ('The Cemetery of Metaphors')
description: A small artistic "graveyard" where clichéd expressions have been buried.

name: Grave of the Hel-Horse
description: According to legend, this is the burial place of a mythical three-legged death omen horse.

name: Eremitageslottet (Hermitage Hunting Lodge)
description: The castle used by the Danish Royal Family as a hunting retreat.

name: Opalsøen (The Opal Lake)
description: Once a granite mine, this beautiful lake is the perfect place to cliff jump and zipline in Denmark

name: Bison of Bornholm
description: Even though bison are not native to this Danish island, you might find them walking around the Almindingen Forestas as part of a nature experiment.

name: Professor Labris Plads (Professor Labri Square)
description: A quirky, larger than life sculpture on an old bunker pays tribute to an eccentric carnival entertainer.

name: 'Bar Roma'
description: This enormous aluminum figure in Denmark lying in a plunged position, echoes the global debate on canceled historical statues.

name: REGAN Vest
description: Deep in the forest of northern Denmark, this secret underground bunker was built at the height of the Cold War.

name: Viking Traffic Lights
description: At these downtown crossings, pedestrian lights show axe-wielding Vikings instead of the usual walking figures.

name: Fandens Fingre (The Devil's Fingers)
description: Local legend has it that the Devil's claw marks are etched into this distinctive rock.

name: Hønekilden (The Hen Spring)
description: This enchanting spring, steeped in local lore and a touch of royal history, was said to be the place where newborns were collected, rather than being delivered by storks.

name: 'Tumlinger på livets vej' ('Tumblers on Life's Path')
description: Four whimsical figures make for an unexpected sight along an urban commuter route.

name: Jomfruspringet (The Virgin Leap)
description: This aged paving stone, marked by a maiden's foot imprint and the tales of her plunge from the cathedral tower, serves as a memorial to a tragic local legend.

name: Jons Kapel (Jon's Chapel)
description: Local legend says a monk brought Christianity to Bornholm through his sermons in a cave inside this free-standing rock.

name: Sea Pink
description: A giant pair of rose-tinted sunglasses offers an illuminated view of Aarhus Bay.

name: 'Min Indre Svinehund' ('My Inner Beast')
description: This striking pig statue was part of a 1993 guerilla art project against racism.

name: Wetting Trap Chair
description: Prank chair from an 18th-century Danish royal court shows the eternal popularity of fart jokes.

name: Franciscan Monastery Museum
description: An underground museum of monks, bones, and medieval ruins.

name: Thorvaldsens Museum
description: Where a neoclassical sculptor rests at the heart of the museum devoted to him.

name: Lenin Statue of Herning
description: Relocated from the Baltics, this bronze figure of the Soviet leader reclines on fork-like supports.

name: Gongen (The Gong)
description: A massive bell inside a Danish library rings to announce the birth of a baby in the city.

name: Defiled Baptismal Font
description: This historical font became infamous after a maid was allegedly tricked into urinating in it, leading to her tragic death sentence.

name: Køge Huskors Memorial
description: This marker commemorates a long-lasting historical haunting and demonic possession that led to one of Denmark's worst witch trials.

name: Slattenpatten and Her Daughters
description: A bronze fountain celebrating a strange, seductive, long-breasted, motherly creature from regional folklore.

name: 'Zygote'
description: A towering sculpture, likened to an adult toy, sparked local and national debate.

name: Skulls of Saint Nicolai Church
description: Hidden in plain sight, the origin of these embedded skulls remains the stuff of legend.

name: Denmark’s Smallest Equestrian Statue
description: Honoring a local founder, this tiny tribute once vanished under mysterious circumstances.

name: ‘The Earth Spirit’
description: An oversized sculpted hand and mooning backside remind visitors that protecting the Earth can come with humor.

name: 'Kløvet sten' ('Split Stone')
description: After attracting attention for nearly three decades while standing in front of a church, this phallic stone sculpture is now tucked away in a campus backyard.

name: ‘Dutch’n’Dutch’
description: This sculpture in Denmark presents Arnold Schwarzenegger embracing his own doppelgänger.

name: ‘Survival of the Fattest’
description: A bold sculpture highlighting the stark inequality between Western affluence and developing world hardship.

name: Møllehøj (Mill Hill)
description: The highest point in Denmark has been a matter of dispute for over 80 years.

name: Bell Chamber of Budolfi Church
description: This bellfry was once said to be the Devil’s lair—or perhaps the home of a mischievous gnome?

name: Den Hvide Jomfru (The White Maiden)
description: Legend speaks of a banished noblewoman allegedly walled inside this little tower.

name: Heksens Hårbånd (The Witch’s Headband)
description: Legend says an angry old witch’s hair ribbon turned to stone, now streaked across the rock she threw at the church.

name: The Swedish Houses
description: After the destruction of WWII, Sweden offered to build some 300 wooden houses on this Danish island.

name: The Russian Cemetery in Allinge
description: World War II Soviet Russian soldiers were buried on the Danish island after the war had ended.

name: Martin Andersen Nexø Museum
description: The childhood home of the author of Pelle the Conqueror.

name: Kulturtårnet
description: A little-known cafe in an old copper bridge tower with 360-degree views of the harbor.

name: Fox Hotel in Copenhagen
description: Hotel designed by indie graphics artists.

name: Royal Danish Naval Museum
description: A museum filled with tiny, adorable versions of the mighty Royal Danish Navy.

name: Aarhus Hjernesamlingen (Aarhus Brain Collection)
description: Over 9,000 brains collected between 1945 and 1982 demonstrate the results of untreated mental illness.

name: Heisenberg's Bathtub
description: Did he come up with a theory of cosmic-ray showers while sitting in this bath?

name: It Wasn't Meant To End Like This
description: Digger attempting to hide itself under 300 tons of rubble.
